preamble: Debuccalization of aspirated stops into the glottal fricative [h] occurs when the oral articulation of the stop is lost, leaving only the aspiration as the phonetic reflex.

examples.note: Proto-Indo-European *p evolved into [h] in Classical Armenian through an aspirated stage (e.g. PIE *pǝtēr > Arm. hayr).
In certain dialectal developments, aspirated stops can weaken and eventually debuccalize.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
